algorithms.title: High-Risk Substance-Related and Eating-Disorder Presentation Field Approach
algorithms.description: Sequence for recognizing and managing high-risk substance-related and eating-disorder-associated presentations.
steps: Assess scene safety and obtain vital signs/glucose regardless of a presumed substance or psychiatric cause.

If severe confusion, autonomic instability, tremor, and hallucinations appear roughly 48-96+ hours after last alcohol use: recognize as possible delirium tremens — treat as a life-threatening emergency with supportive care, IV access, monitoring, and transport.
If pinpoint pupils, respiratory depression, and altered mental status are present: recognize as possible opioid intoxication — prioritize airway/ventilation support.
If agitation, tachycardia, hypertension, diaphoresis, and dilated pupils are present: recognize as possible stimulant intoxication — monitor closely for cardiac risk.

If the patient has a known or suspected eating disorder and reports cardiac symptoms (palpitations, syncope, chest discomfort): evaluate with full medical seriousness, including ECG/monitoring where available.
For personality-disorder-associated presentations: maintain calm, consistent, non-judgmental care without attempting a field diagnosis.
Apply core de-escalation principles with heightened vigilance for rapid deterioration across all high-risk presentations.
